step: Connect to Canton and load the isolated workspace
description: The single loading image establishes that the UI is connecting to a real ledger-backed workspace.
narrationCue: The demo begins by connecting this workspace to Canton.

step: Introduce the private Receivable Sale workflow
description: The welcome panel presents the four-stage product flow before any ledger action.
narrationCue: Use the existing problem statement and introduce CloakRFQ as the institutional workflow.

description: Raw Debtor identity and invoice metadata are visible in the Seller registration view.
narrationCue: The Seller registers a represented Receivable while raw Debtor identity stays private.

description: The progress bar advances and the Seller is prompted to obtain Compliance and Risk attestations.
narrationCue: The Receivable is now on-ledger and ready for scoped review.

description: Compliance controls its two eligibility decisions independently from the Seller.
narrationCue: Compliance now reviews Seller eligibility and RFQ-package eligibility.

description: The eligibility result is recorded while certificate derivation remains a Seller action.
narrationCue: Compliance approval is now recorded as a scoped attestation.

step: Review and select the Receivable risk tier
description: Risk is evaluated by a role separate from Compliance.
narrationCue: The independent Risk Assessor now assigns the certified risk tier.

description: The certified tier is ready for controlled disclosure to invited Funders.
narrationCue: The risk rating is recorded without exposing raw Debtor records to Funders.

description: Both authority-controlled attestations are present, enabling the certificate-backed action.
narrationCue: With Compliance and Risk complete, the Seller can now open the RFQ.

step: Create one private request per Funder
description: The Seller derives both certificates and creates three isolated RFQ requests.
narrationCue: The Seller opens the RFQ, creating one private request for each invited Funder.

step: Review the certified package and compose an offer
description: Funder A sees certified terms, a withheld Debtor identity, and no competing offers.
narrationCue: Funder A can now review its private package and submit an allocation-backed offer.

description: The first offer is recorded with a committed mock CIP-56 allocation.
narrationCue: Funder A submits its Private Quote with committed demo funding evidence.

description: Funder B chooses a different price, recourse model, and notification requirement.
narrationCue: Funder B independently chooses different terms without seeing Funder A.

description: Funder B sees only its own submitted outcome and allocation evidence.
narrationCue: Funder B submits its own funding-backed Private Quote.

description: The Seller compares price, recourse, notification, and funding status while settlement is disabled.
narrationCue: The Seller can compare both offers, but settlement remains locked until the response window closes.

description: The Seller sees contracts needed to originate, compare, and settle the deal.
narrationCue: The same transaction history now appears through separate Canton party views.

description: Funder A sees only its request, Quote, and related allocation state.
narrationCue: Funder A sees only contracts it is entitled to see.

description: Funder B sees its distinct request and Quote, not Funder A data.
narrationCue: Funder B receives a separate private view of the same RFQ.

description: The Coordinator is not a stakeholder on Private Quotes and sees no quote contents.
narrationCue: The Coordinator sees no Private Quote contents.

description: Before settlement, the Auditor has no private RFQ or Quote contracts.
narrationCue: Before settlement, the Auditor sees none of the private marketplace.

description: A non-party sees no useful contract data.
narrationCue: The Outsider sees nothing useful; this is Canton disclosure, not a visual filter.

description: Accept and settle is enabled only after the common response deadline.
narrationCue: The response window is now closed, so the Seller can accept and settle one offer.

description: The Seller receives demo payment and a pending Receivable transfer is initiated atomically.
narrationCue: The selected Quote settles: payment reaches the Seller and a pending transfer is created.

description: The Seller is paid and the winning Funder receives the ownership-acceptance action.
narrationCue: The winning Funder can now accept the pending Receivable transfer.

description: The post-settlement ownership step completes the transfer lifecycle.
narrationCue: The winning Funder accepts ownership, completing the transfer lifecycle.

description: The Auditor sees settlement fields while losing Quotes and raw source data remain withheld.
narrationCue: Only now does the Auditor receive scoped ReceivableSaleSettlement evidence.

description: The Activity log shows real Daml transactions generated during the workflow.
narrationCue: The ledger Activity log provides the transaction-level execution trail.

description: The closing frame restates private competition, funding evidence, and scoped auditability.
narrationCue: Close with the existing value proposition about privacy shaping the marketplace.
